chunks.title: Components Fixture
chunks.description: Render the runtime-facing adapters from @inth/docs in one browser route.
chunks.text:
Components Fixture

Render the runtime-facing adapters from @inth/docs in one browser route.

Components Fixture

Authoring Example

AutoTypeTable still needs extracted type data from the route or conversion pipeline. This demo renders that extracted output on /docs . Quickstart route External reference 1. Author MDX Start with semantic components such as Callout , Tabs , Cards , and TypeTable . 2. Render in TanStack Start Import the .mdx file directly and provide mdxComponents through the shared runtime map. 3. Validate separately Keep AutoTypeTable in pipeline coverage where source extraction actually happens. Package manager Command -- -- npm npx pm add @inth/docs pnpm pnpm dlx pm add @inth/docs yarn yarn dlx pm add @inth/docs bun bunx pm add @inth/docs Overview This tabset proves the package adapters hydrate correctly inside the demo app. Tables TypeTable is safe to render live because all of its data is already present in the MDX payload. Pipeline note AutoTypeTable is not shown live here because extraction depends on a stable build-time file system base path. Property Type Description Default Required -- -- -- -- -- command string Command template with a \ pm placeholder. - ✅ Required commands Record\<PackageManager, string Explicit per-manager overrides when templates are not enough.

```mdx <Callout variant="success" title="Runtime fixture"> Render the exported adapters through your shared `mdxComponents` map. </Callout> <PackageCommandTabs command="{pm} add @inth/docs" /> <Tabs items={["Overview", "Types"]}> <Tab value="Overview">Tabs hydrate in the browser.</Tab> <Tab value="Types">Use `TypeTable` when the type data already exists in MDX.</Tab> </Tabs> <Mermaid chart={`flowchart LR A[MDX] --> B[mdxComponents] B --> C[Rendered route] `} /> <TypeTable type={{ command: { type: "string", description: "Command template with a {pm} placeholder.", required: true, }, }} /> <AutoTypeTable name="PipelineExampleOptions" path="./apps/docs-smoke/type-fixtures/pipeline-example.ts" type={pipelineExampleType} /> ``` ```mermaid `flowchart LR A[MDX fixture] --> B[mdxComponents] B --> C[TanStack Start route] C --> D[Playwright coverage] ```
chunks.codeText: ```mdx <Callout variant="success" title="Runtime fixture"> Render the exported adapters through your shared `mdxComponents` map. </Callout> <PackageCommandTabs command="{pm} add @inth/docs" /> <Tabs items={["Overview", "Types"]}> <Tab value="Overview">Tabs hydrate in the browser.</Tab> <Tab value="Types">Use `TypeTable` when the type data already exists in MDX.</Tab> </Tabs> <Mermaid chart={`flowchart LR A[MDX] --> B[mdxComponents] B --> C[Rendered route] `} /> <TypeTable type={{ command: { type: "string", description: "Command template with a {pm} placeholder.", required: true, }, }} /> <AutoTypeTable name="PipelineExampleOptions" path="./apps/docs-smoke/type-fixtures/pipeline-example.ts" type={pipelineExampleType} /> ``` ```mermaid `flowchart LR A[MDX fixture] --> B[mdxComponents] B --> C[TanStack Start route] C --> D[Playwright coverage] ```

chunks.title: Quickstart
chunks.description: Install and run your first command.
chunks.text:
Quickstart

Install and run your first command.

Quickstart

1. Install Install the package. 2. Import Import convertAllMdx from @inth/docs/convert . 3. Build Run bun run pipeline build . Package manager Command -- -- npm npx pm install @inth/docs pnpm pnpm dlx pm install @inth/docs yarn yarn dlx pm install @inth/docs bun bunx pm install @inth/docs Overview Basic usage is one call to convertAllMdx srcDir, outDir, remarkPlugins . Advanced Pass custom remark plugins alongside defaultRemarkPlugins for extensions and set a stable basePath when AutoTypeTable needs to resolve project files.

chunks.title: Search and AI Answers
chunks.description: Generate a local docs search index and stream source-grounded AI answers.
chunks.text:
Search and AI Answers

Generate a local docs search index and stream source-grounded AI answers.

Search and AI Answers

@inth/docs includes headless search logic for docs sites that want to bring their own UI. ℹ️ Info Demo route Open the live search example at /search. Typing runs local search only; the Ask button is the only action that can call the model.

chunks.title: Search and AI Answers
chunks.description: Generate a local docs search index and stream source-grounded AI answers.
chunks.text:
Search and AI Answers

Generate a local docs search index and stream source-grounded AI answers.

Search and AI Answers

Package Surfaces

Property Type Description Default Required -- -- -- -- -- @inth/docs/search runtime Edge-safe search, answer context, query validation, JSON body limits, and rate limiter helpers. - ✅ Required @inth/docs/search/node build time Node-only \ generateSearchIndex\ helper that reads converted markdown and writes \ docs/search-index.json\ . - ✅ Required @inth/docs/search/ai runtime Vercel AI SDK \ streamText\ wrapper for source-grounded plain text answer streams. - Optional

chunks.title: Search and AI Answers
chunks.description: Generate a local docs search index and stream source-grounded AI answers.
chunks.text:
Search and AI Answers

Generate a local docs search index and stream source-grounded AI answers.

Search and AI Answers

Build the Index

Run conversion first, then generate the search index from markdown The generated index is static JSON. In the demo app, scripts/search-generate.ts copies it into src/generated/docs-search-index.json so routes can import it without reading from the file system at request time.

```ts import { generateSearchIndex } from "@inth/docs/search/node"; await generateSearchIndex({ outDir: "public", baseUrl: "https://docs.example.com", }); ```
chunks.codeText: ```ts import { generateSearchIndex } from "@inth/docs/search/node"; await generateSearchIndex({ outDir: "public", baseUrl: "https://docs.example.com", }); ```

chunks.title: Search and AI Answers
chunks.description: Generate a local docs search index and stream source-grounded AI answers.
chunks.text:
Search and AI Answers

Generate a local docs search index and stream source-grounded AI answers.

Search and AI Answers

Runtime Search

Import the generated JSON and query it from your own route handler Search results include urlWithHash and absoluteUrlWithHash so the UI can link directly to the matched heading. The demo renders matching heading id s with slugifyDocsHeading .

```ts import { searchDocs, type DocsSearchIndex } from "@inth/docs/search"; import indexJson from "./generated/docs-search-index.json"; const results = searchDocs(indexJson as DocsSearchIndex, "package tabs"); ```
chunks.codeText: ```ts import { searchDocs, type DocsSearchIndex } from "@inth/docs/search"; import indexJson from "./generated/docs-search-index.json"; const results = searchDocs(indexJson as DocsSearchIndex, "package tabs"); ```

chunks.title: Search and AI Answers
chunks.description: Generate a local docs search index and stream source-grounded AI answers.
chunks.text:
Search and AI Answers

Generate a local docs search index and stream source-grounded AI answers.

Search and AI Answers

AI Answers

Use streamDocsAnswer when you want a simple Vercel AI SDK integration The answer prompt only includes retrieved docs context, tells the model to cite sources, and asks it to say when the docs do not contain enough context.

```ts import { streamDocsAnswer } from "@inth/docs/search/ai"; const { response } = streamDocsAnswer({ index, query, model: process.env.DOCS_SEARCH_MODEL ?? "moonshotai/kimi-k2.6", productName: "@inth/docs", }); ```
chunks.codeText: ```ts import { streamDocsAnswer } from "@inth/docs/search/ai"; const { response } = streamDocsAnswer({ index, query, model: process.env.DOCS_SEARCH_MODEL ?? "moonshotai/kimi-k2.6", productName: "@inth/docs", }); ```

chunks.title: Search and AI Answers
chunks.description: Generate a local docs search index and stream source-grounded AI answers.
chunks.text:
Search and AI Answers

Generate a local docs search index and stream source-grounded AI answers.

Search and AI Answers

Abuse Protection

1. Search is cheap Debounced typing should call only the local /api/docs/search route. It does not call the model. 2. Answers are explicit Keep model calls behind a button such as Ask , Enter to ask , or Cmd+Enter . 3. Limit request paths Use validateDocsQuery , readJsonWithLimit , getClientIdentifier , and a RateLimiter implementation around public routes. The demo uses an in-memory limiter for local smoke coverage. Production apps should adapt the same RateLimiter interface to a shared store such as Redis, Vercel KV, Cloudflare KV, or Durable Objects.
